Title: Genomic Islands of Pseudomonas syringae pv. tabaci 6605: Identification of PtaGI-1 as a Pathogenicity Island With Effector Genes and a Tabtoxin Cluster

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required genomic-island-associated site-specific excision/integration behavior; implicated in maintenance of PtaGI-1 at its original tRNALys site, indirectly enabling retention of the tabtoxin biosynthetic cluster and virulence capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; PCR detection of excision/circularization/integration junctions; phylogenetic comparison discussed with known GI XerC proteins
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits glutamine synthase and contributes to wildfire disease symptom development and late-stage host colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. xerC is the rightmost core gene in each PtaGI and encodes a site-specific integrase. 2. PtaGI-1 loss abolishes tabtoxin production because the GI carrying the tabtoxin cluster is removed. 3. Sequence divergence of XerC between PtaGI-1 and PtaGI-2 correlates with GI-specific integration sites, suggesting a role in site-specific integration.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not directly mutate xerC alone; its role is inferred from homology to PAPI-1/PPHGI-1 studies and correlation with integration specificity. The experimentally decisive nutrient-metabolism phenotype is at the GI level rather than the single-gene level.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for controlling horizontal transfer or stability of toxin-producing genomic islands in phytopathogenic bacteria.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in bacterial colonies; GI excision/circularization detected by PCR
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence comparative analysis; no accession for omics dataset reported

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with site-specific excision/integration of PtaGI-2, but PtaGI-2 loss does not alter tabtoxin production or virulence in this study
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; PCR detection of GI excision/circularization/integration junctions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. xerC is also present on PtaGI-2. 2. PtaGI-2 undergoes excision and circularization. 3. PtaGI-2 deletion does not affect tabtoxin production, indicating this xerC is not part of the decisive tabtoxin determinant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is experimentally discussed as a core GI gene, but it is not a primary causal gene for nutrient-metabolite change.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for limiting mobility of non-tabtoxin GI elements.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in bacterial colonies; GI excision/circularization detected by PCR
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence comparative analysis; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Tabtoxin biosynthetic enzymes (cluster of 12 ORFs: tblS, tblC, tblD, tblE, tblF, tblR, tabC, tblA, tabA, tabB, tabD, tabP)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tabtoxin toxin biosynthetic cluster within PtaGI-1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits glutamine synthase; major virulence factor contributing to disease symptoms and efficient late-stage colonization in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of the cluster is required for tabtoxin biosynthesis and full virulence; deletion of PtaGI-1 abolishes tabtoxin production and strongly reduces disease symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genomic island deletion (∆PtaGI-1_LR and ∆PtaGI-1_t); tabtoxin bioassay using E. coli growth inhibition with glutamine rescue; plant flood inoculation and infiltration virulence assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtaGI-1 contains a tabtoxin biosynthetic cluster spanning 12 ORFs. 2. Deletion of PtaGI-1 eliminates detectable tabtoxin production. 3. Loss of this cluster coincides with marked reduction in tobacco virulence, supporting tabtoxin biosynthesis as a major pathogenicity determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper validates the cluster at the whole-cluster/PtaGI-1 level rather than functionally dissecting each individual enzyme gene in this study. A representative PCR marker for the cluster was RS24845 in the text, but the cluster itself is described as RS20820-RS20875; likely a typographical inconsistency in the manuscript when referring to the lane 5 marker.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In planta tobacco infection; tabtoxin detection on MG medium with or without glutamine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multi-enzyme conversion of precursor metabolites to tabtoxin through the tab/tbl gene cluster
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant at cluster level; individual rate-limiting step not established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence comparative analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for pathogen control by disabling toxin biosynthesis or monitoring horizontally transferable toxin clusters in epidemiology.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: For the bacterium, loss of the cluster reduces virulence and in planta growth at later infection stages.
